# Globi

> Interactive 3D globe web component for journalism, education, and storytelling.

## What is Globi?

Globi is a browser-native web component (`<globi-viewer>`) that renders interactive 3D globes and flat-map projections using WebGL. It is designed for data journalists, educators, newsrooms, and content creators who need to visualize geographical information interactively.

## Quick Start

```html
<script type="module" src="https://globi.world/globi.js"></script>
<globi-viewer scene="scene.json"></globi-viewer>
```

## Core Capabilities

- 3D WebGL globe and 3 flat-map projections (Azimuthal, Orthographic, Equirectangular)
- 13 celestial bodies (Earth, Mars, Moon, Europa, Titan, and more)
- 5 visual themes (Photo Realistic, Wireframe Shaded/Flat, Grayscale Shaded/Flat)
- Markers with dot, image, model, and text visual types
- Great-circle arcs with animation
- Multi-point paths with dash patterns
- GeoJSON polygon regions with extrusion
- Callout clustering for dense marker sets
- Real-time data binding (ISS tracking, AIS vessel feeds)
- Scrollytelling support via IntersectionObserver
- Full keyboard and screen reader accessibility
- Color-blind-safe 10-color palette
- JSON scene format — zero configuration required
- WYSIWYG visual editor for non-technical creators
- Import/export: JSON, GeoJSON, OBJ
- Internationalization: EN, DE, FR, IT, ES, ZH, AR
- Data source attribution with viewport-aware tracking

## Agent API

Globi exposes `window.globi` with 28 commands:
- Navigation: flyTo, setZoom, resetCamera
- Entities: setMarkers, addMarker, removeMarker, setArcs, setPaths, setRegions
- State: getScene, getMarkers, getVisibleMarkers
- Theme: setTheme, getTheme
- Projection: setProjection, getProjection
- Export: exportJSON, exportGeoJSON, exportOBJ
- Search: search, clearSearch
- UI: toggleLegend, toggleFullscreen

DOM attributes for discovery: `data-globi-body`, `data-globi-projection`, `data-globi-zoom`, `data-globi-marker-count`

## Scene Schema (JSON)

Top-level fields: version, locale, theme, planet, viewerUi, markers[], paths[], arcs[], regions[], animations[], filters[], dataSources[], projection, surfaceTint, overlayTint, calloutCluster

Marker: { id, name: {locale: text}, lat, lon, visualType, color, calloutMode, pulse, category, sourceId }
Path: { id, name, points: [{lat, lon}], color, strokeWidth, dashPattern, animationDuration }
Arc: { id, name, start: {lat, lon}, end: {lat, lon}, color, maxAltitude, animationTime }
Region: { id, name, geojson: {type: "Polygon", coordinates}, capColor, sideColor, altitude }

## Links

- Homepage: https://globi.world
- GitHub: https://github.com/salam/globi
- Editor: https://globi.world/editor/
- Examples: https://globi.world/examples/
